description: Playing the potential higher low reversal, but did not have a key level to work off here and was still counter trend. PA looked pretty good and risk was appropriate
Not a bad set up but had a terrible entry, way too much risk.
Shouldn't have taken this trade, got in out of FOMO and anger. Price was about 670. took 2 entries, second one was worse and was 2 candles later.
Tried the break and retest of 670. Didn't work, but had proper size, lost 9%. Good execution
Took a reject, against rules but had a good entry and tight risk. Sold into EMAs.
Took the trade on half size because it was within risk tolerance but hadn't pulled back yet. Sold early after contracts were not moving well and prev day low was hit.
Looked like it could have been a nice pullback/reclaim on a strong name, SPY was in demand. Should have got in earlier, but was able to keep risk within tolerance. Trade didn't work out.
Took a reject off yesterdays low, set up does not follow my rules but got a really nice entry and took profits well.

Really nice trade, was not in proper size at all because I thought it was going to go higher. Was still within 10% of stop so I should have had one more con. Regardless I executed well and sold at almost LOD.

This was fuckin cooked, I knew it wasn't a good entry and took the trade anyway. And then took it a second time. Just very poor entries overall, don't get filled at the bottom of long wicks like that again. Its almost always going to retest the level and get more liquidity before going. However, right idea the move ended up working out

Chased it a bit on first entry, but got a trim and then stopped at break even. Then waited patiently for a proper entry at 260 and re-entered with proper size. Perfect execution, except took second trim too soon on the full size entry.

Had the right idea here, just got in a little too early because it was unclear where the stop should be. Makes it hard to take the trade, but the idea was correct. Need to keep that in mind for the future. Took a second entry on the next candle after it held it with a wick, but got stopped again, because it was unclear where the stop was.
Not a smart trade, too late in the day and price was moving way too slow. However, risk was tight, I guess the set up was there, so can't be mad. Losses happen.
One of the best trades i've taken in a while because I followed rules perfectly. Good entry, size, and position management.
Usually not a set up I would take, but had the right intentions going in which was to scalp it. I had a good entry and managed it well.
Another stupid trade, there was nothing about the chart or any chart that said go short. Atleast risk was managed.
My worst trade in a while. Entered at 2.8 when I knew the trade wasn't invalidated until cons were at 2.2. Terrible decision there and paid for it with my biggest loss in a while.
Great trade, not the best entry so couldn't get perfect size. Sold way too early, complete numb skull move.

This was a whim wham under 660, META broke out with volume, attracted longs, and then fell back below it. I got filled at a good price but the contracts were extremely spready and volatile. Sold after it failed to confirm under 660. Did a good job not getting filled again while it looked tempting.

Got in a scalp after some bearish price action, got a trim, sold runner at break even. Should have held that last contract because the chart still looked good. Ended up getting back in at a bad average, and then sold that one too.

Even tho its counter trend was willing to take this bounce because it was a huge level, just wanted to see if I could get a knee jerk reaction. Once there was no reaction I sold. Should have waited a bit for first entry, but overall average was good
Got in before the wick, as far as entry and execution go this trade was fine, but I should have waited for a better set up. Jumped the gun a bit, got too excited.

Picture perfect entry, one of the best i've ever had. However I was undersized, should have had another contract since risk was so tight. Sold the last contract early because i had to leave for class, and there was a lot of theta decay.
Testing the gap fill rejection. Trimmed a piece at 6-7%, got stopped on the rest. Got in after candle started wicking, before it turned red.
Great set up, just wrong execution. I should have just held the strong name, instead sold for a stupid trade on SPY. Fear of trade going red, having a bad day lead me to make that wrong read on SPY
Terrible trade not sure what I was doing, I don't take these set ups.
Tried trading the lower low formation under 684, but market was too strong. Should not be looking to short unless we are under VWAP or clearly have exhausted the trend
Nice strong chart with low volume retest of key level, just didn't work out.
Entered with rav, didn't realize how volatile it was, ended up getting 25% in seconds and took profit. Super high risk, should not have taken the trade in hindsight but it worked out.
Got spooked out of this good position because news came out, spy dumped. AAPL never broke structure tho, should have stayed in. Hesitated again on entry
AAPL relatively strong name, holding support while SPY was tapping demand. Sold too early, didn't respect stop, and hesitated on entry.
Went too light, hesitated at entry. This was a great setup, should have had more conviction. Sold one at intraday structure, and then sold the second one way too early, left everything on the table.

Got in half size early, ended up going ~15% but stopped out as SPY touched 686 and TSLA started to bounce. Should have watched for a re-entry or retest though. Missed a really nice set up about an hour later at the same level.

Good set up, nice hourly break and retest holding key level. SPY was at demand and holding it. Only problem was that I hesitated and got a much worse fill than I should have, with less size. Didn't have enough conviction.
Tried the TSLA trade again, not a set up I would normally take, but risk was so tight I figured I may as well follow it with unrav.
This was actually not a bad idea, but lost patience on it and sold. Shortly after it offered a great lower high opportunity that I missed. Entry was not ideal though.
Very nice setup, break and retest over resistence. Got a good entry and got a trim off, unfortunately did not get any follow through so had to stop at break even for a small win.
Another trade I shouldn't have got in, but atleast risk was tight.
Was messing around late in the day, no real reason to take this trade. Looked ok on the 15m chart, but this was not high probability.
Wasn't a terrible set up but definitely not A+. Was playing the breakdown below 676. Had good risk, stopped out of half size at 11%.
Market started forming a base and higher low at demand along with beta names. Went long, took quick first trim due to counter trend, and sold rest into VWAP
Tried the break and retest below 260, but didn't like price action and got out around breakeven.
TSLA was holding 414 key demand, SPY was strong. Got in and got out, conditions weren't the best did not want to re-enter
Stayed patient and found a good set up. got a good entry, didn't get much follow through, saw 15% at highs.
Didn't follow entry rules again => didn't get proper size => didn't get trims off => sold for small loss. Proper entry is everything.
Should not have got in here, got in off FOMO and needed to win a trade. Got scared and sold early.

Not a bad set up, but was not fully convinced due to how aggressive the move was, figured we needed a deeper pullback. I was right, should have waited. Its better to let the whole upside move go and then get an actual nice pull back, or get in after consolidation.
Good set up, but did not follow my entry rules. Got in with 20% risk. If I followed entry rules, I could have trimmed or sold at B/E.
Took this trade purely out of anger
Not a bad trade, just wasn't in a good head space to manage it properly. Set up worked out just couldn't execute.
First order didn't get filled, got pissed and got filled at a higher price. Immediately sold but contracts moved against me and I lost 5%.
Took this trade purely out of anger
Good set up, didn't get continuation but proper entry and only took tiny loss.
Another bad trade, PA was not clean, this was not a strong level, we just bounced off support and made a lower low. No reason to take this trade, got in off emotion.
Bad trade, was not clean PA and got in at a terrible price. Rushed into it
Should not have taken this re-entry, conditions weren't right, it was clearly heavy going back into demand, took out of emotion.
Not a bad trade set up, just didn't work out. Keep in mind that best break and retests touch and go, and this one had a pretty heavy sell candle after the breakout. Regardless, was worth a shot.
Great set up, was hesitant and sold too early. First trim was too soon only about 1/4 R, and sold the last con too soon out of FOMO.
Had the right idea, was a good trade but I was undersized because it didn't go as low as I wanted it to. Took profit at 10% because it started to wick 242 supply, but ended up selling early.
Good patience here, chased it a little bit but it was a good set up, had everything I look for. Was also a little undersized.
Got in on the break of 256, was the correct read, but didn't get the entry I wanted so I got in undersized. Wasn't really clean follow through, got stopped at breakeven.
Poor entry, should have waited for the retest confirmation.
Was trading the 7000 breakdown, but didn't wait for confirmation here. Jumped the gun again, due to built up emotional strain and the need to get back gains.
Set up was good, wanted high of day for TP, didn't get it so got stopped out for a small loss. Nothing wrong here just didn't work out.
Bull flag breakout, didn't get a proper entry, took the trade on emotional tilt, but ended up working out. Got my trail wicked so I didn't get to TP at intended area.
Was not a good trade, didn't have a large enough pullback and beta names were in demand.
Was the right set up and idea, didn't get the immediate follow through, but hit my target.
Got in too early first, and then news came out and I took a much bigger loss than intended with only 1 contract
Got in AGAIN too early, did not wait for a retest. Emotional build up
Counter trend reject, against rules
Went a little too heavy. After the first few wicks when it was clear that risk was going to be hard to define, should have remained more patient or sat this one out.
Good position size, good risk management, good set up, just didn't work out.
Counter trend again, not part of plan.
Bad counter trend trade, even when these trades do workout they are small 10-15% wins that get trailed out. Not worth it.

Entered on 7000 reject. Caught a nice move down to 691 and took profit, but then re-entered looking for continuation. Bad idea to re-enter, it was not a continuation signal until we broke 691, which was the midpoint of the session and VWAP.
Good entry and execution, just bad trade idea. SPY was still holding its demand, so there wasn't enough momentum/confluence for this one to be high probability.
Entered on the hold of 691, trimmed at around 8% due to the higher risk nature of this trade, ended up getting stopped on runners. Not a bad trade, just didn't work out. Managed well.
Great entry, great execution. Held until demand zone. went a bit light because it was higher risk and was in 0 days.
Perfect execution
Took a reject of ES 7000, double top high. Wasn't the best set up because at this point we were up trending on the day. Should have been looking for longs here actually.
Higher risk set up, probably should not have taken. Bounce at demand in a downtrending hourly chart on a red day.
Another bad one.
All indexes were at demand and I was able to get a good entry. No follow through tho, but good execution selling 3/4 at 10%
Correct idea, wrong execution given the conditions. Not fluid price action im gonna get stopped out, and because of the tilt I went heavy.
Another bad trade. Need to read price action and see that its slow, choppy, low volume/volatility. Not good conditions to trade in, don't waste monetary and mental capital.
Good set up, but really bad entry and should have been more strict with my stop.
SPY was holding previous day level at first, went long, but then it broke so I sold. Good execution and position size.
Got a perfect wim wam here. Trapped buyers above breakout level, and on the failure I went short. I did sell a bit early tho.
Break and retest of the 250 level. Had a nice entry and good trade management. Nice trade overall.
Had super tight risk since this was a higher risk entry, stopped out on first entry -$10.
Lower higher formation test. Got stopped on first entry but got back in and made a lot. Took trims too early, but managed the trade well overall.

Had a good read and good entry on this, but underestimated the volatility of TSLA, and ended up getting stopped on multiple trades, and final trade only had 1 contract. Was a good read tho and held the contract until the chart gave me a sell signal. In the future I'll size down and be more patient with entries on TSLA.
Had a great entry, should have just held this or actually added around the 613 level, but sold when it pushed below the wick, looking for re-entry. Stuck to plan, which is the most important
Had the right read here, was a good trade, was undersized because low volume and quad witching. Next week cons did not move at all, should have been 20-30% trade but only got 12%.
Had a great entry, but again was undersized, and then sold way too early. Had some fear because I didn't want to be red from the initial loss, so sold super early. Trade ended up going 75%+.
Had a terrible entry here, which caused me to have to trim at 5%, and then got trailed out. Very bad performance, over traded on the day.
Same thing here as the previous trade. Looked for a bounce, got stopped out by a wick. Need to be more patient and controlled

Nice read on Q's, SPY was also bouncing and both were strong premarket, confirming bullish bias. Q's had better RR with 607 right below. Executed perfectly, took first trim at 13% and then held last contract until the chart gave me a reason to sell.

Wanted to take the bounce of 675. Market was selling hard, I was tilted from NFLX loss, should not have taken the trade without lots of confirmation, especially since it had already bounced there previously.
Didn't have a proper entry here, and I got in before a key level break. Not sure why, but it wasn't a high quality trade. Need to be more disciplined
Good read on GOOGL, strongest chart in the market holding demand while spy was holding 679. Good trade but sold way too early.
Chart looked good intraday, hindsight it was counter trend on the daily. Regardless, still a solid set up, just didn't work out. Entry wasn't optimal.
Good entry but had to go light because it was a bit far from my level. Decided to hold for B/E or LOD, ended up getting stopped at B/E. Good execution, should do that every time.
Break and retest of 679, good entry good trim. Sold runners at break even, good execution. Should have stayed at the screen for another 10 mins to catch that really nice rejection.
Had a great read and great entry on this chart, but sold way too early, missed like 75% of the move. Need to be more patient and understand where the price has room to.

Very strong stock, held 279 demand the previous day, held it again the morning, went long. Was a good idea, but didn't have enough conviction to stay in the trade and got wicked out. This loss triggered emotion that lead to a very bad trading day.
Not a bad set up actually, but had no more mental capital to manage it properly. Sold at 10% and stopped at breakeven.
Break and retest to the down side. Again, don't know why I took this trade. revenge trading.
Extreme revenge trading here. Entered calls when I normally would not have, got stopped, re-entered, trimmed for a small gain, got stopped on runners. Very bad trading and self control.
Had a good read and entry on SPY, but sold way too early. Should have had more size, and should have held longer.

Had a good entry, but trade was mainly a revenge trade because of the loss I had just taken on AAPL. Was not part of the morning plan. was also in too heavy for the set up, these kinds of rejects have to be scaled into.
Tried this trade again, was able to trim some but got trailed out and trade ended up being a loss. was in too heavy once again.
Got in this one way too heavy, was tilted and wanted to make back the gains from previous 2 losses now. Should either had tighter risk or entered lighter.
This was the worst trade of the day, I knew SPY was going higher after that big bounce, but FOMO into a short as soon as I saw the smallest wick. Was not a high probability or disciplined trade.
Bought spy again once it started to fail 684. Should have got 2 contracts. Regardless did a good job holding this one, caught 26%.

Bought this one on the retest of 684, was a good entry but should have had more size. I got the correct entry so I should have had 3 contracts instead of 2. Besides that it was managed pretty well, but should have held longer for first trim. Got trailed out after spy held 682 level.

Got in at a bad average here, so when it went slightly against me I basically freaked out and sold for a loss thinking I should get in at a better average. Should have just held and used my original stop loss, was already in light size, no reason to freak out.
This trade was the right idea, but the worst execution i've ever seen. Took first trim at 3% because I was sweating it, and then trailed out the rest at breakeven. Trade ended up doing 30%.
Absolute monstrosity
This was just a bad trade that I should not have taken. Was an aggressive early entry of the 180 break, but it hadn't broken yet. Some news came and I got stopped out.
This was the right idea, would have been a nice trade. Stopped out and didn't get back in.
Should have bought when this thing was red. Waited for too much confirmation and it hurt my R and position size.

Didn't have a good entry on this one, the best entry would have been at the previous wicks. Couldn't add either because I was in full size. Had to sell for a loss, did a good job selling early and keeping loss small.
Had a good entry, just got stopped out. Nothing I can do just have to look for the re-entry.
Took first trim way too early at only ~9%. Good trade besides that.
This was my best trade of the week, had proper size, good entry, and managed it properly. Didn't end up getting continuation so I sold last runner at break even, but perfect execution.
Wicks started coming in off 225 which was a top watch for me. Ended up getting stopped but idea was right, just had to catch it on the re-entry

Not a great trade, did not wait for the retest. If I had waited for the retest it could have been fine, but lost more than I needed to here and didn't get much confirmation. Market direction was unclear. Don't take trades like these, unless at a minimum the risk is 10%.
Should have added 3 contracts. Was under sized. Also sold too early, sold last contract at 23%, cons went to 45%+. Not very good execution, I was kind of rushed into this trade.

False breakout of 670. This isn't the type of trade I really want to be taking, the stock was just flagging and was strong all day. Sold for tiny gain because I no longer liked the trade, and it ended up being the right choice.
Was looking for the 163 reject, but quickly realized that the stock did not look weak at all, and got out for a small loss. This was mostly a fomo trade, no real reason to enter this one.

Got in early after it showed some signs of bouncing off 597. Then added to the trade at 597, LOD. Did a good job holding until 691 for 40% to take profit. Should have waited longer to take first trim, took it at 12% instead of 15%. besides that perfectly executed, however I wish I looked for another entry around the 601 bull flag.

Second entry off 593, had a nice average, took trims at 8%, sold runners at break even. Wasn't bad, but my idea was correct I just didn't have full conviction in it and was too scared to actually commit to the trade.

Entered a little bit late, was scared to enter right at 592 because I didn't want to see it break the level. Also full sold this one at 5%, was just super scared, cons moved more than expected, and I just took the green.

Nice re-entry here, had a really good entry level. SPY was started to reject its upper range again, NVDA failing but under 180, so the timing was right. I took my first trim too soon. Not upset with where I sold my runner, but I should have re-entered the trade on the next retest of 203 with full size, but did not. Really need to work on fear management. Left tons of money on the table with this one. Idea was correct, execution was off.
Tried this 203 failure on AMD. Entered half size because direction on SPY was unclear, but did not want to miss this trade. Stopped out at the right level, took a small loss. Onto the next

Break and retest of 660 + vwap + PM low, on a weak day in a down trending market. Had a decent entry, but was super scared both entering and managing the trade. Should have added size at my SL, and should have held contracts longer. Only captured about 50% of the profits I should have. Really need to work on fear.
Only got 1 contract because I didn't get the retest, but still a nice trade. Left so much money on the table though. Need to work on this as its become a recurring theme.
Valid trade here to take the reject, it was previous day low while spy was at PDL. Took profits quickly both out of fear and because the market was pretty strong. Had a good entry tho. Starter size.

This was meant to be a swing but ended up hitting my level intraday. Bullish divergence on daily, overall market rallying, and had reclaimed 603 key level. Might have sold too soon, but risk was higher on this because of how much META moves and the price of the contracts. Good trade here, definitely had the correct read on the market being strong.

Break and retest of previous day low + vwap in downtrending day. Had a great entry, bought into a green candle instead of fomo-ing in after a wick. Confirmation was from previous wicks and context. Good trade, should have waited longer to take first trim.

NVDA broke below 194 key demand level, SPY was pretty weak as well. Nice trade, good sizing on initial trade and good position management. When I added back in, I did not get best possible entry, entered on fomo a little after it started selling off. Need to remain patient and objective. Felt a lot of fear adding back in, but the chart looked good, risk was fine, so no need to be scared.

NVDA was wicking above my 194 level, so I decided to try the bounce. Got the best possible entry, risk was very tight so not upset at all about taking this trade. Nice job remaining patient and stopping out when it wasn't going in my favor.
AMD was holding over 243 supply. Was a good trade, correct idea. Should have probably added more size. Could have held longer but with 1 contract can't risk it.

I didn't like the set up, NVDA was weak on the hourly, broke below 184, below VWAP, didn't reclaim it yet, should have actually gone short and not taken the trade at all. And then I exited and re-entered, which caused me to lose more. Had FOMO cuz admins signaled this trade. Need to take a step back, objectively analyze the set up, try and get a nice entry if I'm convicted in the set up.
Nice PA around 239, key level. Had the right idea here, was only able to get $15 out of this but decent trade

Break and retest of 260. Had lots of fomo going into this trade because I had just missed a short on SPY. Ended up getting like the worst possible fill, FOMOing in, and selling extremely early just out of fear. Terrible, rookie execution.

Again had some terrible execution here. First got in, had proper sizing and risk, but then sold early. Got back in on FOMO, sold early again. Then got in a third time, stopped out after trimming at 14%. Trade ended up going 40%+. I just need to take a step back, look at the broader context, and hold with proper sizing.
Tried this reject twice, not really sure why tho. We had the 6900 level, but there was no bearish price action I was just buying puts because of the level. Need to wait for more confirmation.

Entered on a reject, not exactly sure why but I followed admins here. Need to research entry reasons. Average was not very good on the trade, should have added more or at a better point when I had the opportunity. When trimming, should have sold some at 15%, and more at 25%, instead of 12 and 15. Managed runners well.
Tried NVDA over 185 resistance here, I don't think it was the wrong idea, but didn't work out until later in the day and I ended up stopping out.
Went long on AAPL on the reclaim of 260. Idea was correct, ended up working out but I got stopped on this entry and did not pay attention to the re-entry opportunity.
Failure of previous ATH's. Exited early because contracts were not moving in my favor. Should have re-entered and held, but didn't want to since I couldn't properly calculate risk.
Breakdown of QQQ 608 level. Failed it with strength, did a great job of getting a perfect add and sold at the right time.
Second entry on long, SPY was holding VWAP and looking strong, but ended up not working out. Had tight risk on this, happy with execution.

Had 3 entries here, very sloppy execution should have just held original position. Also sold way too early, this was a huge breakout with momentum there was no reason to sell. Need to adjust my stops and risk parameters a bit, have been getting stopped out of good trades way too often.
This was just a trend entry on SPY/early breakout. It ripped off support and then based above VWAP. Went double size on this since I had just taken a double sized loss, and managed the position well.

Went short, and didn't work out. Sized way too large, should have stuck to my own SL of 662.2 but couldn't deal with the fomo if I stopped out and it went, so I just stayed in. Need to be more careful from now on and stick to my stops.

Beautiful sweep of 662 and bounce. Got in a bit late because I was not at computer, but happy I took the trade. Was severly undersized because I was hoping to get a pullback and an add, but executed well with the size I had. Held right into target and broke even on the day.

Went long after a hold of 664 looking for a break and squeeze over 665. Managed risk well but sold a little too early and ended up missing out on gains. But on second entry I followed plan well, took first trim well over 10%, sold runners at break even. Was willing to risk profits for more profits given the break over 665, just didn't work out this time, which is fine

Perfect execution on the entry, Waited for key level to get hit, waited until a wick formed where I could define risk, and then entered with proper size. Exit could have been done much better, took 75% off between 10-17%, should have only sold two here. One at 10-12, one at 17, one at 25-30, and then last one could have held longer as well, but not that upset cuz I still held for 48%. The key is understand the context of the level and market conditions, this was a previous swing high retest on hourly downtrend, so holding longer is called for.

Went short on TSLA after we broke above and then failed 431 key level. First entry was ok, but was a pansy and did not add size at the retest of 431, and ended up being very undersized on a nice trade. Exit was executed well though, was up almost 20%, but decided to hold for 423 or get trailed out, and ended up getting trailed out. Would have been perfect execution if I had added 2 more contracts at 431.
First entry on the spy reject. Got in too early, should have been patient and waited for 659-660 key level that I had charted, which ended up being HOD on the penny. Managed risk well though.
Last entry on SPY. This one worked, but then I got trailed out. hardly had any size on this, took trims at 10% and then sold last contract B/E.

3rd entry, this time it would have worked out, but I had a little too much size and sold a bit early due to fear of losing too much. Key level was not broken, so there was no reason to sell, and this one would have worked out. Improper risk managment combined with fear of losing due to already taking 2 losses
2nd entry on spy, also too early, not gonna take unravelers early entries anymore, just going to wait for key levels to get hit so I can get the highest probability, tightest risk entries.
Took the reject off 662 and NQ 25000, good set up, took trims at 10% but should have followed plan and sold the rest at break even.
Entered on break and retest of 661, had proper position sizing, stuck to plan, took profits about as well as I could have given only 2 contracts.
Entered AMD for 158 bounce with very tight risk, so was able to buy a lot of contracts. Trade worked well, but I did not stick to my profit taking strategy because of fear.

Had the right idea, but hesitated initially and got a bad entry. Didn't manage it properly either, had multiple opportunities to trim at 10% but wanted more because of previous loss. Need to take what i'm given not try and force a big trade that isn't there.
Tried this with tight risk below opening range and 6500. Wasn't a bad idea, followed Mikes signal but held it too long. Still had proper size tho.
Tried this reclaim of 236. Should have entered after previous candle close. Not a great entry, or great contract selection. But kept risk tight and didn't let it eat my mental capital.
Went short after a break and failure of ~6508. Had a great entry, tight risk, proper sizing. Should have held a little bit longer, but overall 8/10 management. Key here was the good entry price.

Plan here was to take a 646.5 bounce. Had planned for 646.5 as risk, but was following rav and he unexpectedly decided to hold for a whole DOLLAR lower than what he said. In the moment I didn't want to miss the move so I held, but from now on I will be following my intended stop and then re-entering if necessary.
Bought AAPL on a close over 240, but then sold when it broke back under. Did not want to lose a lot here, was willing to take the small loss and then re-enter if I got confirmation again.

Another bounce entry. This time I had no mental capital left, and just got out when I was break even on the day. Left some money on the table because I had no will power left to hold. Need to consider this before each trade moving forward.

re-entry on SPY at 644 level. Sized bigger because of the previous large loss. Only made 52 because of poor trade management. I was greedy when up because I wanted to make back the previous loss, and held runners too long, ended up giving back like $50. Can't get greedy, just have to protect gains. Small wins, small losses, big wins.
Re entry on AAPL over 240. Got kinda lucky by selling right at HOD, but besides that it was a good trade and did a great job position sizing. Made almost double what I lost on the first trade.

Reclaim and hold of my level. I shouldn't have taken this trade I was just trying to make something happen after a couple big losers. Ended up not working. Better to just trade other names, I don't know metas levels very well.
Again, got a bad entry on this BnR of 233. Should have recognized I didn't get the best entry and just left it alone.

Wanted to test AMZN over my previous day high level. Ended up getting about the worst fill you can get, and then getting stopped for -86. Had good position sizing but a bad entry. Should have just followed Bretts lead on this.

TSLA was rejecting 334, wasn't a bad set up I just need to be more patient with these and try to get a better fill. Just because I can enter it and keep my risk less than $100 doesn't mean its a good entry. The stock was strong, VWAP was right below, should have just stayed patient on this one. But risk was managed.

Got in because my level started getting wicked. AAPL had a strong catalyst. Not mad about this trade at all tbh, sucks that it didn't work but its a small, managed loss and the set up was there. Properly sized risk because the bottom wasn't totally defined.

This was an emotional trade for sure, but I couldn't miss it. SPY had just wicked me out, which is the main cause of my revenge trades. I got back in with less size, and also at a somewhat poor entry point. Should have added at the lows. I sold this thing way way way too early, but given that I was in class it wasn't too bad. Main regret here is not having a 4th contract, which was needed in this situation to make up for the loss I had just taken.

This one was a nice set up, a clear sweep of liquidity below a key level. Only thing I would change is getting better entry and add points, as my risk was a bit over $100 on this, because I didn't get good adds. Future reference, need to take 2d expirations late in the afternoon.

Nice re-entry on AAPL with proper size. Sold this one a bit too early, and was too SCARED to add some more size on the reclaim/retest of 235. Presented a nice opportunity, but again I was too scared, missed out on a really nice move.
This was a purely emotional trade. Although the idea was there, I had no reason to take this trade and only took it because I was mad about missing the move on my first NVDA short.

This one again was an emotional trade, but a nice set up. I was just so scared when entering this trade that I sold way too early again, gave back another $30. This trade also would have worked out nicely.

Was a great idea, an early entry with tight risk into a potential NVDA breakdown of strong level. NVDA was very weak already. However I got wicked out, should have given it a little more room. Also need to take into account the risk of mental capital when taking a trade like this and seeing myself get wicked out, because it caused me to make a lot of bad decisions.

Was a risky reject given how strong AMD was the whole day, but 162 was a very strong level. I stopped out near breakeven even though I could have caught a decent scalp if I had held. I think I entered this trade based off fomo/over confidence from the previous big win. I went very small size, kind of just itching to take a trade. Lots of fear and hesitation entering it.

Took a SPY trade in the morning. Risk was $80, which is proper sizing, accounting for losing more than LOC. Ended up getting close to 40% on final trim, managed the position well. First trim was 15%+. Did a great job holding this one until it got near supply.
Took AAPL when it broke below 229 and the whole market was weak. Started with a small position and then made 1 add, was waiting for confirmation. Trade didn't work out but I managed risk well.

NVDA got very weak below 177 which was a breakdown level on my watchlist. Initially it was forming a strong wick, so I didn't want to get filled, but then failed completely and so I got filled. It had moved quite a bit and was a bit of a chase, so I was only able to get 1 contract, but glad I took the trade. It was part of my trade plan, I had proper risk, and I held it until it almost hit my target, but it was managed well.

Tried bouncing TSLA off 350. first entry didn't work out, neither did second entry. Managed risk well on both, just can't do Rav style of trading. Too choppy. Should have went short after that bearish break followed by low volume green candle, textbook BnR.

Kind of a cooked trade because we were right above 230, but idea was an early breakdown of AAPL 230. First entry did not work out, lost $66. Second one did workout, but I was frustrated and got greedy, stayed in the trade even though it did not look good at all, ended up giving back some gains for only a small win.
2nd entry, went with a full size to make up for half size loss, but ended up losing here too. Losses are part of the game, and I managed risk well, so can't be upset.

Nice reclaim trade on SPY. Sold off, reclaimed 646, reclaimed VWAP, and then I went long. Had very tight risk, was able to size up and get 6 contracts because of it. Only saw a 7% gain on this, sold too early. Was not in a good emotional state, was feeling nervous and wanted to lock in profit. Should have been objective and noted the low volume on the pullback, still maintaining VWAP, and held a runner. Still a decent trade tho.
Breakdown of a key level. Ended up getting greedy because I was frusterated about my last loss, and held my final runner past break even. Gave back gains.

1st entry on AAPL
Was looking for the build and break above 230, as it was holding 229.5 very well.
Stopped out on my first position which was smaller size. Lost 81
Re entered when it reclaimed 229.5 with one additional contract, ended up only making 92 though because of trims.

Re entered when it reclaimed 229.5 with one additional contract, ended up only making 92 though because of trims. 2nd entry in screenshot. Should have waited longer to take first trim. Atleast until previous HOD. But did a good job of atleast making back the money I lost on the first trade.

Went short because we were below key support and wicking off it bearishly. Nobody else took this trade which makes me think my analysis may have been wrong, even though I was able to catch a scalp. Need to review that. Position sizing was spot on though.
Was patient, sized properly, and took profits well. Could have waited a bit for first trim, but almost perfect execution. Waited for price action confirmation.

Had the right idea, price action was bearish at supply level, but got stopped out a little early and did not want to get back in because of slow movements, and Trump speaking. Still, glad to see I used proper position sizing and had the right read. Just need to execute better next time. Need to size lighter with a loser stop on those slower moving rejects.

This one was close to perfect execution, had the proper sizing just took first trim a little too early, and should have used a market order to stop out at breakeven. Tried limit orders but lost some money because I couldn't get filled while price was dropping. Nice job though.

This was the 3rd entry, bought 1 contract more than I should have, this is because I had just taken 2 winning trades and had FOMO because I sized too small. Need to track this and make sure I keep it under control

Tested the reject of 637 supply with bearish price action to confirm. Went 1/2 size on this one because I only wanted to risk half of my profits for the day, which is fine. Took profit near 635 for a 33% win. Good job holding this one until a key level was hit.

This was the 4th trade, could have maybe added 1 more contract, but also fine with 2. So proper position sizing and did a great job holding runners until a proper level was hit. First trim at 15% second trim at 40%. This was just a reclaim of a key ES level, with bullish price action to confirm.
This was the 1st entry. Was scared and hesitant getting into this one, should have had double the position size I did have, and then should have sold 3/4 contracts at 15%.
AAPL broke under 233 briefly and then reclaim and held it as support. Had a good entry, managed pretty well, but left a lot of money on the table, and was too scared to get full size.
Lost on this spy trade, but was a good trade, and my risk was defined, so it fit within my system and sometimes a loss happens. Just need to manage losses.

Took PLTR short on low volume retest of 184.5, previous day high and previous swing high. GOOGL was a strong name, broke out over a key supply, SPY and QQQ also broke above their resistances. Same set up as TSLA day prior. Left a disgusting amount of money on the table, but it was the right trade to be in.

caught TSLA breakout trade over 312. First entry was a bit late so I only got in with 1 contract, second entry was higher risk so I also only got in with 1 contract. 628 bounce was part of the plan, but need to wait for further confirmation on bounces. Confirmation came later after selling climax occured, and a low volume retrace back to that level and then rally continued.
Spy built over intraday pivot, offered scalping opportunity. Was kind of high risk, probably wouldn't take it again, but it worked out. Needed another contract tho to have consistent position sizing.
